definition: Spot commodity prices are a fundamentally misleading proxy for investable returns. GSCI spot return 2005-2025 was approximately -10% cumulative while GSCI total return was approximately -35% cumulative, with roll yield explaining 60-80% of the divergence. Collateralized commodity futures averaged +5.1% annualized (1970-2007) then -3.2% annualized (2008-2025).
evidence: GSCI spot return ~-10% cumulative vs. total return ~-35% cumulative (2005-2025)
Roll yield explains 60-80% of spot-to-total return divergence
open_questions: Can physical tightening restore positive roll yield despite current AUM levels?
Is the post-2008 roll yield decline permanent or regime-dependent?
